qa_pairs.question: How is the SLURM Raw Shares displayed when a single account is selected in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: When a single account is selected for display, the SLURM Raw Shares is shown as a horizontal red line on the usage figure.

qa_pairs.question: How can I toggle the display of SLURM Raw Shares on the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: You can toggle the display of SLURM Raw Shares using the 'Display allocation target by default' item in the 'Parameters' panel, or by clicking on 'SLURM Raw Shares' in the legend to the right of the figure.

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'Queued jobs' metric represent in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Queued jobs' metric represents a sum of all resources in pending jobs at each time point.

qa_pairs.question: How do I display or hide the 'Queued jobs' metric on the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: You can toggle the display of the 'Queued jobs' metric by clicking on the words 'Queued jobs' in the legend to the right of the figure.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find interactive options for navigating the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: Native Plotly interactive figure options are made available at the top right of the figure window when your mouse pointer hovers over the figure.

qa_pairs.question: What interactive navigation tools are available when hovering over the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: When hovering over the usage figure, you can access icons for 'Download plot as a png', 'Zoom', 'Pan', 'Box Select', 'Lasso Select', 'Zoom in', 'Zoom out', 'Autoscale', and 'Reset axes'.

qa_pairs.question: What information is displayed when I hover over a bar item in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: When hovering over bar items in the figure window, a hover text appears providing the 'User Name', 'Day', and 'Usage' quantity of the specific item under the pointer (note that this returns the usage quantity for the specific user not the sum across users for the day).

qa_pairs.question: How are SLURM Raw Shares represented for rrg-* or rpp-* allocation accounts?
qa_pairs.answer: The SLURM Raw Shares of an allocation rrg-* or rpp-* account are represented as a straight line that corresponds to the account's resource allocation on the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Are SLURM Raw Shares dynamic for all account types?
qa_pairs.answer: No, for default accounts, the SLURM Raw Shares are dynamic over time based on the number of active accounts on the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the benefit of plotting the SLURM Raw Shares for a default account?
qa_pairs.answer: Plotting the SLURM Raw Shares of a default account on a specific cluster is an easy way to determine the expected usage that can be achieved by a default account on that given cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the SLURM Raw Usage metric?
qa_pairs.answer: The SLURM Raw Usage is a metric that the scheduling software uses to determine the priority of accounts.

qa_pairs.question: How is the SLURM Raw Usage calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: The SLURM Raw Usage is the cumulative sum of the account's usage in billing units plus a half-life decay period.

qa_pairs.question: Why would I plot an account's SLURM Raw Usage?
qa_pairs.answer: Plotting an account's SLURM Raw Usage is a convenient way to assess how past usage influences the account's priority over time.

qa_pairs.question: What rule of thumb indicates an account's usage is at par with its target share?
qa_pairs.answer: A good rule of thumb is that if the SLURM Raw Usage is at 10 times the SLURM Raw Shares, then the account's usage is at par with its target share.

qa_pairs.question: What information does the usage figure display regarding clusters and time?
qa_pairs.answer: The usage figure shows your usage for a single cluster over a specified time interval.

qa_pairs.question: How do I select a specific cluster to view usage data for?
qa_pairs.answer: You can select a specific cluster using the 'System' pull-down menu, which contains entries for each of the currently active national clusters that use Slurm as a scheduler.

qa_pairs.question: How can I change the time interval displayed in the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: You can change the time interval displayed in the figure using the 'Start date (incl.)' and 'End date (incl.)' fields in the 'Select system and dates' panel.

qa_pairs.question: What types of jobs are included in the usage figure's display for a selected time interval?
qa_pairs.answer: The usage figure includes all jobs on the specified cluster that were in a running (R) or pending (PD) state during the selected time interval, including both the start and end date.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I select an end date in the future for the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: Selecting an end date in the future will display a 'projection' of currently running and pending jobs for their requested duration into the future.

qa_pairs.question: How is the future projection visually indicated on the usage figure?
qa_pairs.answer: If you select an end time after the present time, the figure will have a transparent red area overlaid on the future time labelled 'Projection'.

qa_pairs.question: What assumptions are made for running jobs in the projection period?
qa_pairs.answer: In the projection period, each running job is assumed to run to the time limit requested for it.

qa_pairs.question: What assumptions are made for queued jobs in the projection period?
qa_pairs.answer: For queued resources, the projection supposes that each pending job starts at the beginning of the projected time (that is, right now) and runs until its requested time limit.

qa_pairs.question: Is the projection feature in the usage figure a forecast of future events?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the projection feature is not intended to be a forecast of actual future events.
